exemplar.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, clear focus on the prescribed title throughout the essay
Effective and specific examples from both Mathematics and Economics that illuminate the central claim
Coherent, well-structured arguments linking simplifying assumptions to both insight and error
Demonstrated awareness of alternative perspectives and acknowledgment of model limitations
Occasional consideration of implications for policy and practice
weaknesses: Limited explicit TOK framing: key concepts like knowledge questions, ways of knowing and areas of knowledge are under-used
Depth of critical evaluation is moderate; counter-arguments are noted but not rigorously interrogated
Evaluation of sourcesâ credibility and reliability is superficial
Conclusion is formulaic and does not fully reconcile earlier nuance with the final stance
TOK terminology and reflection on the role of the knower are only implicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the strand-level feedback placed the essay in the 7â8 band, the examinerâs award of 9 reflects the essayâs sustained engagement, precise illustrations and coherent structure, all of which meet the top-band descriptors. The student consistently links Mathematics and Economics examples back to the title, shows clear understanding of how assumptions both empower and constrain models, and acknowledges alternative viewpoints. Minor lapses in explicit TOK framing, depth of source evaluation and a formulaic conclusion limit the work from a perfect 10, but overall the essay demonstrates the sustained, implication-driven analysis characteristic of high-level performance and justifies a final score of 9.

annotations.comment: The student clearly states the prescribed title, but the essay would benefit from explicitly linking this title to a defined knowledge question and incorporating TOK terminology (e.g. ways of knowing, areas of knowledge) to frame the investigation.
Footnotes list sources without evaluating their relevance or credibility; integrating a brief critique of each sourceâs reliability would demonstrate TOK awareness of evidence quality.
The missile trajectory example vividly illustrates model flaws, yet the essay omits how expert refinement (e.g. simulations) mitigates errorsâa key implication for understanding model usefulness.

The thesis identifies an evaluation of usefulness, yet it does not introduce the role of the knower or clearly articulate the central knowledge question, leaving the line of inquiry underâspecified.

Effective use of a dictionary definition establishes context, but the essay does not interrogate the sourceâs authority or consider how definitions may vary between disciplines, missing a critical TOK perspective.

The Poisson distribution is a wellâchosen example, but the analysis could be deepened by quantifying model error or citing specific deviations to illustrate how âwrongâ the model can be in practice.
The explanation of ceteris paribus shows understanding of economic assumptions but omits alternative perspectives (e.g. behavioural economics) that challenge rationalâactor models.
Introducing GDP as a model is relevant, but the essay proceeds immediately to flaws without framing GDP as a knowledge claim; defining it in TOK terms would clarify its epistemic role.
The Ireland data example is effective, but the essay omits discussion of how cultural or political contexts shape the interpretation of GDP, a key consideration in TOK analysis.
Citing World Bank data underscores institutional authority, yet reflecting on how trust in such institutions affects our acceptance of statistical models would deepen the TOK evaluation.

The selection of Economics as the second area of knowledge is mentioned but justified only superficially; explicitly linking it to TOK concepts such as methodology or the knowledge framework would strengthen the rationale.
The overview of mathematical models is largely descriptive; to develop a stronger argument, introduce a specific example here and discuss how its assumptions affect knowledge claims in mathematics.

While limitations are acknowledged, the essay does not explore how these affect the validity or reliability of resulting knowledge claims; a TOK discussion distinguishing these concepts would strengthen the critique.

The comparison between mathematics and human sciences is a useful link, yet it remains descriptive; contrasting the certainty of mathematical proofs with the probabilistic nature of economic models would enhance evaluation.
The comment on graphical models oversimplifying reality is too brief; linking this observation to how abstraction influences shared mathematics knowledge would improve coherence.

Recognising that simplifications aid early problemâsolving is strong, but the essay should explicitly tie this back to the knowledge question and consider whether simplification constrains the scope of knowledge claims.
The pedagogical source on toy models is appropriate, yet the essay fails to assess its academic credibility or purpose in knowledge formation, missing a TOK evaluation of authority.

The supplyâandâdemand diagram is well drawn, but the essay does not explicitly connect the visual to the central claim or analyze how graphical representations can obscure underlying economic assumptions.
The caption accurately describes the figure, but integrating commentary on how this model exemplifies Boxâs notion of âusefulnessâ would strengthen its relevance to the thesis.
The luxuryâgoods example effectively challenges rationality assumptions; to deepen analysis, the student could explore consumer psychology perspectives and their implications for model reliability.
The Wired article provides a contemporary viewpoint, yet the essay should address potential media bias and evaluate how popular sources inform TOK discussions of models.
Identifying income distribution as a flaw is insightful; comparison with alternative measures (e.g. Gini coefficient) would allow a more nuanced evaluation of each modelâs usefulness.

The summary recaps AOK comparisons but becomes repetitive; synthesizing insights to show how the balance between simplicity and accuracy structurally affects different areas of knowledge would improve coherence.
The paraphrase of the title in the conclusion contains a minor grammatical inconsistency and reads as formulaic; refining this statement would enhance clarity and precision.

Revisiting Poisson and supplyâdemand in the conclusion is redundant; a stronger ending would explicitly link your final judgment to the original knowledge framework and discuss broader implications.

The final statement rejects the title claim but contradicts earlier nuance on model usefulness; aligning your concluding stance with prior analysis and acknowledging complexity would improve coherence.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
